items.title: Exploratory market segmentation
items.description: Analysts cluster customers on behavioral features, inspect the dendrogram for natural merge gaps, and define campaign tiers at different cut heights without committing to a single k upfront.

items.title: Bioinformatics sample grouping
items.description: Hierarchical clustering orders genes or patients in heatmaps, surfacing treatment-response subgroups and guiding hypothesis-driven follow-up experiments.

items.title: Taxonomy and content organization
items.description: Embedding vectors of documents or products are clustered hierarchically to build nested navigation trees for search, recommendations, or internal knowledge bases.

items.title: Anomaly context via small clusters
items.description: Points that merge only at very high dissimilarity appear as late singleton branches—useful for flagging outliers relative to the bulk structure of the dendrogram.
